insights: Establishes module-level documentation for the entire menu app with comprehensive overview
Documents critical architectural rules: tenant isolation via organizationId, soft delete pattern, base variant requirement
Sets default_app_config pointing to MenuConfig for backward compatibility
Declares module scope: Menu, Category, Product, Allergen, Theme entities
quality_indicators: Clear enumeration of critical business rules

insights: Prepared structure with commented example implementations for all admin classes
Defines consistent admin interface patterns: list_display, list_filter, search_fields, readonly_fields
Includes queryset filtering pattern for soft deletes (deleted_at__isnull=True)
Multi-tenant filtering capability documented for applicable models
Uses fieldsets for logical grouping of fields in Menu admin
quality_indicators: Consistent pattern application across multiple models
Admin interface design follows Django best practices
deferred_implementation: Allergen and Theme admin classes lack tenant isolation in example (models not yet implemented)
All @admin.register decorators commented out pending model creation

insights: Defines AppConfig subclass with all required Django configuration parameters
Sets name='apps.menu' to match import path exactly
Configures BigAutoField for all auto-generated primary keys
Implements ready() method hook for future signal registration
Includes commented signal import pattern for when signals module is created
quality_indicators: Forward-looking design with signal registration placeholder

patterns_discovered.description: All models use deleted_at timestamp field for soft deletion instead of physical deletion
evidence: admin.py admin classes filter with deleted_at__isnull=True
Consistent across all entity types (Menu, Category, Product, Theme)
patterns_discovered.architectural_significance: Enables audit trails, recovery options, and maintains referential integrity

patterns_discovered.description: Tenant isolation enforced through organizationId inclusion in all queries
evidence: __init__.py critical rule: 'Every query MUST include organizationId for tenant isolation'
Admin filtering examples include organization in list_filter
Search fields include organization__name for context
patterns_discovered.architectural_significance: Mandatory security requirement for multi-tenant SaaS application

patterns_discovered.description: Consistent admin interface pattern applied across all models
evidence: All admin classes use same field configuration structure
Ordering configured based on entity type (created_at, sort_order, name)
patterns_discovered.architectural_significance: Provides consistent user experience in Django admin panel

patterns_discovered.description: Layered documentation approach: module-level, class-level, and method-level docstrings
evidence: Detailed module docstring in __init__.py with critical rules
AppConfig has class and method docstrings explaining purpose
Admin.py includes comprehensive header and example implementation comments
patterns_discovered.architectural_significance: Facilitates onboarding and reduces future maintenance burden

patterns_discovered.description: Core structure created with placeholder comments showing how to implement when models exist
evidence: admin.py contains complete commented example code for all admin classes
apps.py ready() method shows commented signal registration pattern
Clear TODO-like structure without using TODO comments
patterns_discovered.architectural_significance: Reduces rework and provides implementation roadmap

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: AppConfig name parameter must match import path exactly
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Name already correctly configured; would fail app loading if mismatched

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Admin registration depends on model existence
gotchas_discovered.context: All @admin.register decorators are commented out, will fail if uncommented without models
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear comments indicating dependencies; prevent accidental uncommentation

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Soft delete filtering must be applied in all admin get_queryset methods
gotchas_discovered.context: Pattern shown in examples but not yet enforced systematically
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consistent pattern documented in admin examples; easy to copy-paste
Still functional for backward compatibility; will not cause errors; can be removed in future Django versions

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Multi-tenant filtering must be applied at model level, not just admin
gotchas_discovered.context: Admin examples show filtering but actual model querysets not yet implemented
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document that model managers must enforce organizationId filtering for production

what_was_accomplished: Created menu Django app structure with proper AppConfig
Defined application scope and critical business rules
Prepared admin interface with example implementations
strategic_value: Provides clear roadmap for model creation in next subtasks
Establishes consistent patterns to follow throughout app development
Reduces decision-making in future development phases
Prevents future rework of admin interfaces
approach_outcome.readiness_for_next_phase: HIGH - Models can now be created following the documented patterns
approach_outcome.technical_debt: MINIMAL - Well-structured foundation with no shortcuts taken

recommendations.recommendation: Create model managers with @property methods enforcing organizationId filtering
recommendations.justification: Soft delete and tenant isolation patterns documented at admin level but must be enforced at model querysets to prevent data leaks

recommendations.recommendation: Create a BaseModel abstract class encapsulating soft delete and multi-tenant patterns
recommendations.justification: Reduces repetition across all models; ensures consistency of critical security requirements

recommendations.recommendation: Document the admin example pattern comment syntax for team consistency
recommendations.justification: Other apps may follow this deferred implementation pattern; standardizing syntax aids navigation

recommendations.recommendation: Create signals module pre-emptively with signal definitions for menu lifecycle events
recommendations.justification: ready() method already prepared for signal registration; avoids future import errors
recommendations.implementation_timing: When creating models that require signal handlers

recommendations.recommendation: Remove default_app_config in future Django version upgrade (3.2+)
recommendations.justification: Deprecated but functional; document for future cleanup

recommendations.recommendation: Implement custom admin base class with soft-delete filtering baked in
recommendations.justification: Reduces boilerplate in admin.py when models are registered; ensures consistent filtering behavior
